questions.question_text: Which one of the following is the principal psychoactive constituent present in tobacco that causes dependence in smokers over time?

options.text: Caffeine
Nicotine
Ethanol
Morphine

questions.explanation: Nicotine is the addictive alkaloid in tobacco that stimulates dopamine release in the brain's reward pathway, producing tolerance and dependence; withdrawal from it drives continued smoking.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Caffeine is found in coffee and tea, not tobacco.
Ethanol is the psychoactive agent in alcohol.
Morphine is derived from opium poppy, not tobacco.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following substances acts as a central nervous system depressant rather than as a stimulant in the human body?

options.text: Amphetamine
Cocaine
Alcohol
Ephedrine

questions.explanation: Alcohol (ethanol) is a CNS depressant that slows neural activity, impairs coordination, judgement and reaction time; amphetamine, cocaine and ephedrine are classical stimulants that excite the CNS.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Amphetamine boosts alertness and is a stimulant.
Cocaine is a powerful CNS stimulant of abuse.
Ephedrine raises heart rate and alertness as a stimulant.

questions.question_text: Blood doping by transfusion is banned in sport primarily because it produces which of the following unfair physiological advantages for endurance athletes?

options.text: Greater muscle strength in short sprints
Increased red blood cell count and oxygen delivery
Improved coordination of fine motor skills
Lower body temperature during long events

questions.explanation: Blood doping, whether autologous or homologous transfusion or via EPO, raises haemoglobin mass and red cell count, boosting oxygen-carrying capacity and aerobic performance in endurance events like cycling and distance running.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Strength in sprints is helped by anabolic agents, not transfusion.
Fine motor skills are unaffected by red cell volume.
Core temperature is not reduced by blood doping.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following best describes a Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) granted under the anti-doping rules to an athlete with a genuine illness?

options.text: A permanent immunity from all future dope tests
Prior approval to use a prohibited medication for a documented medical condition
A cash reward for successfully passing drug tests
A licence to supply banned drugs to teammates

questions.explanation: A TUE is a formal, time-limited permission granted by a TUE Committee allowing an athlete to use a prohibited substance or method when medically necessary, with no reasonable permitted alternative and no extra performance gain.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: TUEs never confer permanent immunity from testing.
No financial reward is attached to TUEs.
TUEs authorise personal medical use, not supply to others.

questions.question_text: Social drinking of alcohol is linked to harm at the community level chiefly through which one of the following patterns of consequence?

options.text: Higher rates of road accidents, domestic violence and productivity loss
Improved literacy levels and school attendance
Decreased rates of cancer across the population
Stronger immunity to seasonal infections

questions.explanation: Alcohol misuse is a major driver of drunk-driving crashes, interpersonal and family violence, workplace absenteeism and health-system costs, making it a leading modifiable cause of social harm globally.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Alcohol is associated with poorer, not better, school outcomes.
It increases several cancer risks rather than lowering them.
Alcohol weakens rather than strengthens immune defence.

questions.question_text: Long-term anabolic steroid abuse in adolescent male athletes is most likely to produce which of the following combinations of adverse effects?

options.text: Premature closure of growth plates and testicular atrophy
Improved bone density and delayed puberty
Reduced acne and calmer mood states
Enlarged lungs and thickened skin healing

questions.explanation: Anabolic androgenic steroids in adolescents can fuse epiphyseal plates prematurely, stunting adult height, and suppress endogenous testosterone production leading to testicular shrinkage, gynaecomastia, acne and mood swings.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Steroids worsen acne rather than reducing it.
Puberty can be accelerated and distorted, not delayed.
Lung anatomy is not enlarged by anabolic agents.

questions.question_text: Under the 2021 WADA Code, a first intentional Anti-Doping Rule Violation involving a non-specified prohibited substance typically attracts an ineligibility period of which duration?

options.text: Six months
One year
Four years
Lifetime ban automatically

questions.explanation: The 2021 WADA Code sets a standard four-year period of ineligibility for a first intentional violation involving non-specified substances; it can be reduced to two years only if the athlete proves the violation was unintentional.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Six months is far below the standard sanction.
A lifetime ban applies only to repeat serious offences.
One year is too short for intentional doping.

questions.question_text: Which of the following best captures the reason human Growth Hormone (hGH) is prohibited at all times on the WADA list for athletes?

options.text: It only functions during competition hours
It increases lean muscle, aids recovery and has no legitimate performance role
It is harmless but expensive to test
It is found naturally in food and cannot be measured

questions.explanation: hGH stimulates protein synthesis, increases lean body mass, reduces fat and accelerates tissue recovery; its effects persist beyond detection windows, so WADA bans it in and out of competition under S2 peptide hormones.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: hGH acts continuously, not only during competition.
hGH has serious health risks; it is not harmless.
hGH is tested through specialised biomarker assays.

questions.question_text: A sprinter tests positive for a diuretic despite having no reason to reduce weight for competition; why are diuretics prohibited on the WADA list?

options.text: They directly build muscle mass
They act as masking agents by diluting urine and concealing other drugs
They provide extra oxygen to working muscles
They replace electrolytes lost in training

questions.explanation: Diuretics such as furosemide increase urine volume and dilute metabolites of anabolic agents or stimulants, thereby masking other banned substances in samples; they are listed as S5 masking agents banned at all times.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Diuretics do not build muscle tissue at all.
They do not increase oxygen delivery in any way.
They actually flush out electrolytes rather than replace them.

questions.question_text: Which of the following rationales most comprehensively explains why society invests heavily in anti-smoking public health campaigns directed at the youth?

options.text: Tobacco advertisements are visually unappealing
Youth initiation predicts lifelong addiction and disease burden preventable early
Tobacco is easy to detect in saliva tests at schools
Cigarettes are always cheaper than other leisure goods

questions.explanation: Most adult smokers begin before age 18, and early initiation strongly predicts lifelong nicotine dependence, cardiovascular disease, lung cancer and COPD; preventing uptake in youth gives the highest public-health return on investment.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Advertisement aesthetics are irrelevant to policy rationale.
Saliva testing in schools is not the reason for campaigns.
Price alone does not drive anti-smoking public programmes.

questions.question_text: Match each substance or method in Column A with the WADA Prohibited List code or category in Column B and select the correct combination from the options.
questions.explanation: Nandrolone is an anabolic androgenic steroid (S1); EPO is a peptide hormone (S2); furosemide is a diuretic/masking agent (S5); blood transfusion is a prohibited method under M1 manipulation of blood components.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: EPO is a peptide hormone, not a steroid.
Furosemide is a diuretic, never a method.
Blood transfusion is a method (M1), not a substance.

questions.question_text: Match each harmful health condition in Column A with the substance most strongly associated with causing it listed in Column B and pick the correct option.
questions.explanation: Alcohol is the classic cause of cirrhosis; smoking causes COPD via chronic airway damage; heroin (an opioid) overdose suppresses breathing and kills; stimulants like cocaine can trigger hypertensive and cardiac emergencies.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: COPD is a smoking-driven airway disease.
Cirrhosis is overwhelmingly alcohol-related.
Heroin is an opioid, linked to respiratory overdose deaths.

questions.question_text: Match each anti-doping term in Column A with its most accurate definition listed in Column B, then choose the option showing the correct mapping.

column_b.text: Electronic record tracking biological markers over time
Group of athletes subject to whereabouts testing
Principle holding athlete responsible regardless of intent
Lab report of a prohibited substance in a sample

questions.explanation: An AAF is a positive lab result; the RTP is the elite group subject to whereabouts; the ABP tracks haematological and steroidal markers longitudinally to detect doping patterns; strict liability pins responsibility on the athlete.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: The AAF is a lab result, not a principle.
The ABP is a biological record, not a test pool.
Strict liability is a principle, not a pool of athletes.

questions.question_text: Read the assertion and reason regarding the Athlete Biological Passport programme and select the option best describing their logical relationship.
questions.assertion: The Athlete Biological Passport can detect doping even without a single positive test for a specific drug.
questions.reason: It monitors longitudinal variations in blood and steroid markers that indirectly reveal doping activity.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: The ABP flags unusual shifts in parameters like haemoglobin, reticulocyte count and steroid ratios across time within one athlete; abnormal profiles can establish an anti-doping rule violation without a direct positive sample.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: The reason directly explains how ABP flags doping.
Both statements reflect established anti-doping practice.
The assertion is well-documented in WADA publications.

questions.question_text: Examine the assertion and reason about alcohol in sport and choose the option that correctly describes their logical connection.
questions.assertion: Alcohol is generally harmful to sports performance despite its short-term relaxing effect on the athlete.
questions.reason: It impairs coordination, reaction time, dehydrates the body and disturbs sleep quality and recovery.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: Alcohol slows neural processing, reduces balance and precision, acts as a diuretic causing dehydration, suppresses REM sleep and blunts protein synthesis, all of which impair training adaptation and game-day performance.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: The physiological reasons listed directly explain poor performance.
Both statements are well-supported by sport science.
Alcohol's ergolytic effects are widely documented.

questions.question_text: Consider the assertion and reason regarding caffeine's current status in sport and select the option showing their correct logical relationship.
questions.assertion: Athletes may legally consume caffeine before competition without risking a doping sanction today.
questions.reason: Caffeine is classified under WADA's S6 stimulants category and therefore carries a mandatory ban in competition.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: Caffeine is indeed legal for athletes today, but the reason is wrong: caffeine was moved off the Prohibited List in 2004 and now sits on the Monitoring Programme, not under S6 stimulants; it carries no sanction.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Caffeine is clearly not banned, so assertion is true.
The reason's S6 classification for caffeine is incorrect today.
Both statements cannot be true together.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following stages of the typical progression of substance abuse in an adolescent into the correct chronological order as observed clinically.

items.text: Problem use affecting studies and family

options.text: Q → P → S → R
P → Q → R → S
S → R → Q → P
Q → S → P → R

questions.explanation: Substance abuse typically progresses from experimental use, to regular use with tolerance, to problem use affecting life domains, and finally full dependence marked by tolerance, craving and withdrawal symptoms on cessation.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Experimental use precedes regular use always.
Dependence is the final, most severe stage.
Problem use follows tolerance before full dependence.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following steps of a school-level doping prevention and education programme for student athletes in the correct logical implementation sequence.

items.text: Conduct baseline knowledge assessment of students
Evaluate programme outcomes and revise content

options.text: Q → P → S → R
P → Q → R → S
S → R → Q → P
P → S → Q → R

questions.explanation: Good programme design identifies the audience first, assesses baseline awareness, delivers targeted educational interventions and workshops, then evaluates outcomes so content can be revised for the next cycle.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Stakeholder identification logically precedes assessment.
Evaluation is the closing step, not the first.
Workshops follow rather than precede needs analysis.

questions.question_text: State whether the following statement is true or false: Passive or second-hand tobacco smoke exposure is harmless to non-smokers sharing the same room environment.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Second-hand smoke contains many of the same toxins and carcinogens as directly inhaled smoke; long-term passive exposure raises risks of lung cancer, heart disease, asthma in children and sudden infant death syndrome.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: Second-hand smoke contains carcinogens identical to direct smoke.
Children exposed passively face higher asthma rates.
Heart disease risk rises even in non-smoking spouses.

questions.question_text: Study the diagram showing the WADA anti-doping testing and results management flow and identify the step at which the athlete may request a B-sample analysis.

options.text: Before the sample is collected from the athlete
After notification of an Adverse Analytical Finding from the A-sample
Only after the final hearing is concluded
During the initial whereabouts filing process

questions.explanation: Once the A-sample yields an Adverse Analytical Finding and the athlete is notified, they have the right to request a confirmatory B-sample analysis before disciplinary proceedings progress to the hearing and sanction stage.
questions.subject: Physical Education
elimination_hints: A B-sample cannot be requested before any sample exists.
Whereabouts filing is logistical, unrelated to B-sample rights.
Waiting until after the hearing defeats the purpose of B-sample.
